platform.name: React Native
platform.category: Frameworks, mobile, games, hardware, and creative ecosystems

assumptions: React Native Get Started recommends Expo and does not require a Meta developer account for the tutorial path.
No Expo account, paid EAS plan, or app-store account is assumed for Create your first app.
Node.js, editor, Expo Go, and a physical device may still be required by the Expo tutorial.

developer_goal: Follow React Native's recommended Expo path to create StickerSmash, run it, edit the first screen, and observe the update.

first_success.outcome: The Expo React Native project runs and shows the tutorial's edited Home screen on connected targets.
first_success.observable_signal: Connected apps show a dark #25292e screen with centered white Home screen text after saving app/index.tsx.

route_selection.surface: React Native Get Started → Expo Create your first app tutorial
route_selection.rule: Prefer the vendor-recommended framework path (Expo) over bare React Native, but resolve peer Snack and Expo-account boundaries before verifying.

candidates.name: Recommended Expo tutorial: Create your first app
candidates.reason: React Native recommends Expo for new apps and links this bounded chapter, but peer Snack and SDK-template choices remain consequential.

candidates.name: Introduction interactive Snack only
candidates.reason: Shorter browser-only edit exists on Introduction, while Environment setup moves getting-started to Expo.

candidates.name: React Native without a Framework (Android Studio / Xcode)
candidates.reason: Documented as a non-recommended alternate for unusual constraints.

required_path.action: Install Node.js LTS, a code editor, and Expo Go on a physical Android or iOS device.
required_path.observable_result: Prerequisites for create-expo-app and Expo Go are available.

required_path.action: Run npx create-expo-app@latest StickerSmash and select the SDK 54 template when prompted.

required_fields.label: Expo SDK template (SDK 54)
required_fields.notes: Tutorial states the prompt is temporary during an SDK transition.

required_path.observable_result: StickerSmash project directory is created with dependencies installed.

required_path.action: Download tutorial assets, replace assets/images, open the project, and run npm run reset-project.
required_path.observable_result: Project matches the tutorial baseline file layout.

required_path.action: Start the Expo development server, open the project on the device via QR code, and open web with W.
required_path.observable_result: The app is running on the physical device and web.

required_path.action: Replace app/index.tsx with the documented React Native component and styles, then save.
required_path.observable_result: Connected mobile and web apps show the dark Home screen text.

prerequisites.description: Node.js LTS, a code editor, a developer machine with a terminal, and Expo Go on a physical device.
Familiarity with TypeScript and React as assumed by the Expo tutorial.

external_gates.description: Physical device must allow installing Expo Go from the corresponding app store.

unavoidable_waits.description: create-expo-app dependency install and Expo bundler startup.

platform_outcomes.description: create-expo-app scaffolds the StickerSmash Expo Router project.
Expo applies the saved app/index.tsx change to running clients.

excluded.item: Expo Application Services (EAS) and paid plans
excluded.reason: Optional services after the framework getting-started path.

excluded.item: Later Expo tutorial chapters after Create your first app
excluded.reason: First chapter ends after the saved Home screen verification.

excluded.item: Bare React Native without a Framework setup
excluded.reason: Documented alternate, not the recommended new-app path.

uncertainties.question: How should the corpus account-creation starting boundary map for React Native when the recommended Expo tutorial does not require Expo or Meta account creation?
uncertainties.impact: Blocks verified status under the account-creation contract.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Corpus policy for frameworks whose first success is local/tooling-only without vendor signup.

uncertainties.question: Is the Introduction Snack edit or the local Expo StickerSmash chapter the selected first success?
uncertainties.impact: Snack eliminates project, package, device, and asset steps; Expo matches the Environment setup recommendation.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Explicit lexicographic selection between Snack and Expo Create your first app under SELECTION-POLICY.md.

uncertainties.question: Will the required SDK 54 template prompt remain stable?
uncertainties.impact: Template choice changes Expo Go compatibility and generated files.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Current Expo tutorial confirmation after the temporary SDK transition ends.
